006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with

008 *

010 *

016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.rest.server.observation;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.AssertionUtils.*;
020
021import java.util.concurrent.atomic.*;
022
023/**
024 * Static facade over an explicitly-installed default {@link Observer} for ergonomic custom
025 * (non-request) observations.
026 *
027 * <p>
028 * {@code juneau-rest-server} has no method-interception / AOP substrate, so custom observations are
029 * <b>explicit</b>: wrap the block of work in a try-with-resources over {@link #observe(String, String)}.
030 * This mirrors how the request path is instrumented (the framework opens / closes the observation around
031 * the {@code @RestOp} handler) but lets application code instrument arbitrary work.
032 *
033 * <h5 class='topic'>Installing a backend</h5>
034 *
035 * <p>
036 * The default observer is {@link Observer#NOOP} until a backend is installed via {@link #install(Observer)}
037 * &mdash; typically once at startup, after the application's {@code MetricsRecorder} / {@code TracerHook}
038 * beans are known. Until then (and in any process that never installs one) {@link #observe(String, String)}
039 * hands back {@link Observation#NOOP} and costs nothing.
040 *
041 * <p class='bjava'>
042 *    <jc>// At startup, once the observability beans are built:</jc>
043 *    Observations.<jsm>install</jsm>(<jk>new</jk> Observer(<jv>metricsRecorder</jv>, <jv>tracerHook</jv>));
044 *
045 *    <jc>// Anywhere in application code:</jc>
046 *    <jk>try</jk> (Observation <jv>o</jv> = Observations.<jsm>observe</jsm>(<js>"order.load"</js>, <js>"team=payments"</js>)) {
047 *       <jk>return</jk> loadOrder(<jv>id</jv>);
048 *    } <jk>catch</jk> (RuntimeException <jv>e</jv>) {
049 *       <jv>o</jv>.setError(<jv>e</jv>);
050 *       <jk>throw</jk> <jv>e</jv>;
051 *    }
052 * </p>
053 *
054 * <p>
055 * Code that prefers explicit dependency injection over a global can hold an {@link Observer} directly
056 * (resolved from the {@code RestContext} bean store) and call {@link Observer#start(String, String)} &mdash;
057 * this facade is a convenience, not the only entry point.
058 *
059 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
060 *    <li class='jc'>{@link Observer}
061 *    <li class='jc'>{@link Observation}
062 * </ul>
063 *
064 * @since 10.0.0
065 */
066@SuppressWarnings({
067   "resource" // observe(...) returns the Observation handle for the caller's try-with-resources; closing it here is wrong.
068})
069public class Observations {
070
071   // Constant name intentionally uses camelCase to match observation/metrics naming conventions.
072   @SuppressWarnings("java:S115")
073   private static final String ARG_observer = "observer";
074
075   private static final AtomicReference<Observer> defaultObserver = new AtomicReference<>(Observer.NOOP);
076
077   private Observations() {}
078
079   /**
080    * Installs the process-wide default {@link Observer} used by {@link #observe(String, String)}.
081    *
082    * <p>
083    * Intended to be called once at application startup. Passing {@link Observer#NOOP} (or calling
084    * {@link #reset()}) restores the no-op default.
085    *
086    * @param observer The observer to install. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
087    */
088   public static void install(Observer observer) {
089      defaultObserver.set(assertArgNotNull(ARG_observer, observer));
090   }
091
092   /**
093    * Resets the process-wide default observer back to {@link Observer#NOOP}.
094    */
095   public static void reset() {
096      defaultObserver.set(Observer.NOOP);
097   }
098
099   /**
100    * Returns the currently-installed default {@link Observer}.
101    *
102    * @return The default observer; {@link Observer#NOOP} if none was installed. Never <jk>null</jk>.
103    */
104   public static Observer observer() {
105      return defaultObserver.get();
106   }
107
108   /**
109    * Starts a custom observation against the installed default {@link Observer}.
110    *
111    * @param name The observation name &mdash; used as both the span name and the metric timer name.
112    *    Must not be <jk>null</jk> or blank.
113    * @param tags Additional metric tags as comma-separated {@code key=value} pairs, or <jk>null</jk> /
114    *    empty for none.
115    * @return The started {@link Observation} (a try-with-resources handle). Never <jk>null</jk>.
116    */
117   public static Observation observe(String name, String tags) {
118      return defaultObserver.get().start(name, tags);
119   }
120
121   /**
122    * Starts a custom observation with no additional tags.
123    *
124    * @param name The observation name. Must not be <jk>null</jk> or blank.
125    * @return The started {@link Observation}. Never <jk>null</jk>.
126    */
127   public static Observation observe(String name) {
128      return defaultObserver.get().start(name, "");
129   }
130}
